serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 ml (250 g) of prepared multigrain cereal drink, typically made by dissolving one sachet or 2–3 tablespoons of powder in hot or warm water.

nutrient_source.title: Multigrain Cereal Drink (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Multigrain Cereal Drink (Less Sugar), record ID D04010007. All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat and fatty acid fractions not reported by HPB; set to -1. Potassium not reported by HPB; set to -1 with documentation in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific multigrain cereal drink (less sugar) formulation in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 55 (low; boundary value at the low/medium threshold of ≤55) based on the closest analogous entry: instant oat-based cereal drink with moderate sugar content, GI approximately 55 per Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Per the University of Sydney GI classification, GI ≤55 is categorised as low. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (55 × 27.1) / 100 ≈ 14.9, rounded to 15.0. Carbohydrate per serving = 10.84 g/100g × 250 g = 27.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar present (4.93 g/100g); low dietary fibre (0.67 g/100g); sodium moderate at 36 mg/100g
hypertension.guidance: This 'less sugar' multigrain cereal drink still contains added sugar (4.93 g/100g) and very low fibre (0.67 g/100g), which are insufficient to support blood pressure management; individuals with hypertension should choose genuinely unsweetened multigrain cereal powder (sugar = 0 g) and verify potassium content is adequate (≥100 mg/100g) before regular consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with added sugar (4.93 g/100g); carbohydrate 10.84 g/100g with minimal fibre (0.67 g/100g); estimated GI 55 (low boundary)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Despite 'less sugar' labelling, this beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and causes glycaemic spikes due to 10.84 g/100g carbohydrate with only 0.67 g/100g fibre; individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this drink and choose genuinely unsweetened multigrain cereal powder or water-based alternatives with no added sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar (4.93 g/100g) contributes to cardiovascular risk; low saturated fat; calcium (76 mg/100g) is a minor positive
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages, including 'less sugar' variants, is associated with increased cardiovascular risk through triglyceride elevation and insulin resistance; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this drink and prefer unsweetened whole-grain alternatives with higher fibre content.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar promotes pro-inflammatory pathways; no omega-3, polyphenols, or meaningful antioxidants present; fibre only 0.67 g/100g
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This drink provides no anti-inflammatory benefit and the presence of added sugar may promote low-grade inflammation; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should choose unsweetened multigrain variants or beverages with added anti-inflammatory ingredients such as turmeric or ginger.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Multigrain Cereal Drink (Less Sugar) is a processed instant beverage popular in Singapore, made by dissolving a powder blend of multiple grains — typically wheat, oats, brown rice, barley, and soy — in hot or warm water. It is marketed as a 'less sugar' variant of the standard multigrain cereal drink, meaning it contains reduced but not zero added sugar (approximately 4.93 g per 100 g of prepared drink). The drink has a mild, lightly sweet, toasted grain flavour with a smooth, slightly viscous texture. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast drink or morning snack and is widely available in Singapore supermarkets and convenience stores in sachet or tin formats. Nutritionally, per 100 g of prepared drink it provides 51 kcal, 0.77 g protein, 0.47 g fat, 10.84 g carbohydrate, 4.93 g sugar, 0.67 g dietary fibre, 36 mg sodium, 76 mg calcium, and 0.23 mg iron. The calcium content (76 mg/100g) is a notable positive, likely from added calcium carbonate fortification. Despite the 'less sugar' label, this beverage is still classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) for clinical purposes because added sugar is present. The low fibre content (0.67 g/100g) means carbohydrates are rapidly absorbed, making this drink unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes or those managing blood glucose. The estimated glycemic index is approximately 55 (low boundary per University of Sydney classification), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 15 per standard 250 ml serving. For hypertension management, the drink is not recommended due to the presence of added sugar and low potassium. For cardiovascular health, regular consumption of SSBs including reduced-sugar variants is associated with increased cardiovascular risk through triglyceride elevation and insulin resistance. The drink is vegetarian, vegan, halal, and dairy-free, but contains gluten (wheat, oats, barley) and soy allergens.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (record D04010007), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. All per-100g macros and key micronutrients are taken verbatim from this exact record. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008, clearly documented. Saturated fat, fatty acid fractions, and several micronutrients are not reported by HPB and are set to -1 with documentation.
notes: 1. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for D04010007 (Multigrain Cereal Drink Less Sugar) as of 2026-04-16. 2. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium is not reported in the HPB record for this item; no confirmed approved-source value available, therefore set to -1 pending laboratory analysis or updated HPB data. 3. GI is an analogous estimate of 55 based on the closest approved-source equivalent: instant oat-based cereal drink with moderate sugar content per Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008), International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values, Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283, University of Sydney GI Database. Per the University of Sydney GI classification scale (low ≤55, medium 56–69, high ≥70), GI 55 is categorised as LOW. gi_category corrected from 'medium' to 'low' to reflect this boundary classification. No direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the approved GI sources. 4. Dietary tags from input (wholegrain, low_sodium, low_calorie) are noted: 'low_sodium' is defensible at 36 mg/100g; 'low_calorie' is defensible at 51 kcal/100g; 'wholegrain' reflects the grain blend composition. 5. This food is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) for clinical purposes despite the 'less sugar' label, as added sugar is present (4.93 g/100g). 6. All core macros (protein_g = 0.77, fat_g = 0.47, carbohydrate_g = 10.84) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification is required. 7.
